jokes.text: Frank, no one understands the subtleties of Charlie's retardation better than me.
jokes.setup: Mac offers to help Frank navigate Charlie's confusing map
jokes.punchline: Claims expertise in understanding Charlie's mental deficiencies
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Mac's delusional self-importance combined with casually offensive language typical of the show's dark humor

jokes.text: Then you will be handsomely rewarded.
jokes.setup: Mac continuing his grandiose offer to help Frank
jokes.punchline: Speaks like he's offering a quest reward in a fantasy game
jokes.explanation: Mac's tendency toward dramatic, overly formal speech in mundane situations

jokes.text: 'Cause I'm your big brother, dude, and I'm the boss of you.
jokes.setup: Mac suddenly asserting authority over Dennis
jokes.punchline: Using childish playground logic to establish dominance
jokes.explanation: Mac's pathetic attempt at asserting masculinity through juvenile behavior

jokes.text: I will yank your underwear over your head so hard, your asshole will rip in half.
jokes.setup: Dennis threatening Mac about the noogie
jokes.punchline: Escalates wedgie threat to graphic anatomical consequences
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation from playground threat to graphic imagery

jokes.text: Mac, can an asshole rip in half? Like tissue paper.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking about Dennis's threat
jokes.punchline: Mac's matter-of-fact response treating it as legitimate anatomy question
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Charlie's innocent curiosity and Mac's confident ignorance

jokes.text: Take the cash register. Take the girl.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to cooperate with what he thinks are robbers
jokes.punchline: Casually offers up Dee as if she's property
jokes.explanation: Dennis's casual misogyny and self-preservation instinct

jokes.text: But they're not trying to take me anywhere! Don't try to be a hero. Do what they say! They're not saying anything.
jokes.setup: Dennis insisting Dee go with captors
jokes.punchline: Dee pointing out the logical flaw in his panic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of confusion with Dennis's illogical panic

jokes.text: And a Planet Hollywood jacket. A leather one. Reversible. And we need a-a reversible Planet Hollywood jacket too.
jokes.setup: McPoyles making ransom demands
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and outdated fashion demand, then Ryan copying it
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd demand - specific, dated, and impractical. Ryan's echo adds to the dysfunction

jokes.text: They can't get a Planet Hollywood jacket. Those places don't even exist anymore. They don't make those jackets. They'd have to call Stallone, and he's not gonna give his up. Maybe Aykroyd.
jokes.setup: Discussing the impossibility of the ransom demands
jokes.punchline: Escalating from business logistics to celebrity name-dropping
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about dated pop culture, escalating to absurd celebrity logistics

jokes.text: Oh, my God. What is that? Does that start with a sore throat? Because I think I might be getting it.
jokes.setup: Mac mentions Stockholm syndrome
jokes.punchline: Dee thinks it's a physical illness with symptoms
jokes.explanation: Dee's ignorance played for laughs - treating psychological condition like a cold

jokes.text: Mac or Charlie, right? I mean, what do they have to live for? Not one thing.
jokes.setup: Discussing who should die instead of them
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual dismissal of his friends' lives
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment - narcissistic and callous toward his supposed friends

jokes.text: He had a great life. Yeah. Sure. He had a full, full, good ride. Oh, a fantastic ride. I had a terrible ride. Charlie, you had the worst ride. You deserve another shot at the ride. I want a better ride. Oh, you oughta ride and ride.
jokes.setup: Justifying why Dennis should die instead
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd 'ride' metaphor with Charlie's enthusiastic participation
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with the 'ride' metaphor becoming increasingly absurd, Charlie's innocent enthusiasm

jokes.text: It's too late for the guys, but there's plenty of time to save me.
jokes.setup: Mac calling Frank on walkie during crisis
jokes.punchline: Only concerned with his own survival, writing off his friends
jokes.explanation: Mac's self-preservation instinct overriding any loyalty to friends

jokes.text: I volunteer to rest first. Bullshit. You can't volunteer to rest.
jokes.setup: McPoyles assign digging work shifts
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately trying to avoid work, Mac calling him out
jokes.explanation: Dennis's immediate laziness and entitlement, Mac's rare moment of calling him out

jokes.text: It's making me crazy, dude. Hold on. Oh, my God. He's burning up. Sit down. You gotta sit down. Oh, God. I'm getting sick.
jokes.setup: Mac claims Stockholm syndrome is making him paranoid
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately developing 'physical symptoms' of the psychological condition
jokes.explanation: Charlie's suggestible nature making him think he has physical symptoms of psychological condition

jokes.text: So, guys, do you mind if I grab a couple of beers? I feel we would be a lot more relaxed if we had some beers. Oh, and a cooler! Get a cooler. We're gonna want to keep the beers cold.
jokes.setup: Charlie claiming Stockholm syndrome symptoms
jokes.punchline: Uses 'illness' to request beer and progressively more elaborate beer storage
jokes.explanation: Charlie's manipulative use of fake illness to get what he wants, escalating demands

jokes.text: She's a deaf-mute. The only reason she raised her hand is 'cause you guys did. Really? Let's try that again, right?
jokes.setup: Margaret voting with the hostages
jokes.punchline: Revealing she just copies hand gestures, then Mac testing it
jokes.explanation: Good visual setup with the voting, then Mac's childish fascination with testing the mimicking

jokes.text: Hey, I wonder what else we could get her to do? Uh, let's blink really fast. Oh, come on. That's awesome.
jokes.setup: Mac discovering Margaret copies actions
jokes.punchline: Both getting excited about making her perform like a trained animal
jokes.explanation: Shows the characters' casual cruelty and childish fascination with control

jokes.text: If here by now... then bad place be. Trouble time for you... when heat comes.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to read Charlie's map/warning
jokes.punchline: Charlie's broken English warning that makes little sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - his bizarre way of writing warnings in broken syntax but somehow ominous

jokes.text: Why are you covering your mouth? So she can't read my lips, dude. I don't want her reading lips. Her? She can't read lips. You don't think? No way.
jokes.setup: Mac covering mouth while talking about Margaret
jokes.punchline: Mac's paranoia about lip reading from a deaf-mute who just mimics gestures
jokes.explanation: Mac's illogical paranoia combined with his misunderstanding of disabilities

jokes.text: You should be paying attention, Charlie, because this could be very good for you. Come on, come on. You're losin' me. Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to explain inheritance scheme
jokes.punchline: Charlie's complete disinterest in what should be life-changing news
jokes.explanation: Charlie's short attention span even for potentially life-changing information

jokes.text: With Frank dead, the will gone, Charlie gets all the money. I get all the money? What money?
jokes.setup: Mac explaining inheritance scheme to Charlie
jokes.punchline: Charlie having no idea what money Mac is talking about
jokes.explanation: Charlie's complete obliviousness to the inheritance plot driving the episode

jokes.text: Where's the real map? The real map is tattooed on my body. Get outta here. Where? Right here.
jokes.setup: Charlie revealing fake map, Mac asking for real one
jokes.punchline: Charlie claiming it's tattooed on his butt
jokes.explanation: Setup for gross-out humor, relies on shock value more than wit

jokes.text: I can't see anything. Look closer. What do you see? Nothing. What do you smell? Oh, my God! Really? You fart in my face?
jokes.setup: Mac reluctantly looking at Charlie's butt for the 'map'
jokes.punchline: Charlie farts in his face instead
jokes.explanation: Classic lowbrow humor - fart joke with setup, but relies on gross-out more than cleverness

jokes.text: Why? 'Cause you stole my map, and Frank's in the vents! You're lyin', we could die, and I never got to fart in your face before!
jokes.setup: Mac asking why Charlie would fart on him
jokes.punchline: Charlie's innocent logic about bucket list priorities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Charlie's childlike logic about life priorities in crisis

jokes.text: We don't want your milk, Liam. Then start breakin' bricks, wet nips.
jokes.setup: Liam offering milk to hostages
jokes.punchline: Liam's bizarre threat using 'wet nips' as an insult
jokes.explanation: Iconic McPoyle line - absurd, memorable threat that became a fan favorite quote

jokes.text: Ryan, stab somebody! Oh! What have you done? Give me the milk. Showin' 'em we're not bluffin'. No, you're supposed to stab one of them. You stab one of them!
jokes.setup: Liam ordering Ryan to stab a hostage
jokes.punchline: Ryan stabs Liam instead, creating chaos
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection and escalation showing McPoyle incompetence and dysfunction

jokes.text: Get inside their head. And have sex with them. Mm-hmm. Have sex with them? No. I was gonna say, pit them against each other.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining manipulation strategy
jokes.punchline: Dee immediately jumping to sex, Dennis having to clarify
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke showing Dee's one-track mind and Dennis's horror

jokes.text: I just need to get alone with her and let the pecs do the talkin'.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining his seduction plan
jokes.punchline: Believes his muscles will seduce Margaret
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac delusion about his sexual appeal and muscle obsession

jokes.text: Whatever happens, make sure Charlie's the first one to die. What? Why? Because of this will business. He stands to gain the most.
jokes.setup: Frank giving Mac secret instructions
jokes.punchline: Wants Charlie killed for financial reasons despite their friendship
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual betrayal of Charlie for money, despite their close relationship

jokes.text: So once the McPoyles take out Dennis, Dee and Frank... Yeah. Then you and I will split the money right down the middle. Ooh, ooh, ooh-ooh. That's kind of a lot, don't you think? I'll tell you what. I'll give you 25%.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Mac planning to split inheritance
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately trying to negotiate Charlie down from 50% to 25%
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Mac's greed overriding partnership deal

jokes.text: Frank has to die. Gotta kill Frank. Gotta kill him somehow.
jokes.setup: Charlie processes Frank's betrayal
jokes.punchline: Immediately flips to planning Frank's murder with cheerful determination
jokes.explanation: Charlie's casual shift to murder planning shows his simple logic and loyalty reversal.

jokes.text: No, I don't. No! I just think it's a good way to decide who to kill first. I'm tryin' to put myself in their position, you know? It's called compassion, dickheads.
jokes.setup: Others accusing Dee of Stockholm syndrome
jokes.punchline: Dee claiming her bloodthirsty suggestions are 'compassion'
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Dee calling murder suggestions 'compassion' while denying Stockholm syndrome

jokes.text: Get on your knees, bitches! What are you doing? Now you're on their side? You wanna be a McPoyle, trash bag?
jokes.setup: Dee fully embracing captor role
jokes.punchline: Dennis's horror at her transformation, calling her 'trash bag'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dee's Stockholm syndrome, Dennis's disgusted reaction

jokes.text: Do you mind? Margaret, you like sweat, don't you? It is 'Margaret,' isn't it? Of course, it is. You know, your eyebrow... It drives me crazy. It's so thick. It's so... dark... and so very... connected.
jokes.setup: Mac attempting to seduce Margaret
jokes.punchline: Calling her unibrow attractive in the creepiest possible way
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Mac's delusional seduction attempt treating obvious flaws as attractive

jokes.text: You're a stone cold fox, Margaret. You're a stone cold fox, and I want you. I gotta have you. I need you. I want you inside me.
jokes.setup: Mac continuing his seduction attempt
jokes.punchline: Escalating to increasingly desperate and confused sexual language
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Mac's desperation and confused sexuality

jokes.text: I am gonna shove my fist right into your ass, hard and fast... not in a sexual way... in a 'I am pissed off at you' way...
jokes.setup: Mac threatening Frank over walkie
jokes.punchline: Having to clarify his violent threat isn't sexual
jokes.explanation: Mac's homoerotic threats requiring clarification, typical of his repressed sexuality

jokes.text: It's where he goes to be alone and break bottles. Dude, you gotta be real careful. He's gonna find you.
jokes.setup: Frank asking about Charlie's 'bad room'
jokes.punchline: Mac's matter-of-fact explanation of Charlie's weird anger room
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Charlie's bizarre coping mechanisms

jokes.text: But that goddamn Mac, he... Then tell me, Frank. What do you think about... this?
jokes.setup: Charlie confronting Frank about betrayal
jokes.punchline: Charlie revealing he found Frank's will first
jokes.explanation: Good reversal with Charlie getting the upper hand over Frank

jokes.text: Bye, Dad!
jokes.setup: Charlie apparently about to kill Frank
jokes.punchline: Cheerful goodbye as if leaving for school, not committing murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - innocent delivery of dark content, great economy of words

jokes.text: That was very... hot and sweaty and passionate... milky.
jokes.setup: Mac describing his encounter with Margaret
jokes.punchline: Adding 'milky' as if it's a normal romantic descriptor
jokes.explanation: Mac's disturbing romanticizing of the McPoyle aesthetic

jokes.text: I like men in tight, white underpants.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to prove her McPoyle loyalty
jokes.punchline: Blurting out sexual preference for McPoyle aesthetic
jokes.explanation: Dee's desperate attempt at Stockholm syndrome leading to awkward confession

jokes.text: I love inbreeding! I like men in tight, white underpants.
jokes.setup: Dee tries to prove her McPoyle loyalty
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic endorsement of taboo practices and McPoyle fashion
jokes.explanation: Dee's complete Stockholm syndrome transformation. Shocking but somewhat cheap shock value.

jokes.text: Yippie-kayyay, Mr. Fuckin'... Brother!
jokes.setup: Charlie doing action hero entrance
jokes.punchline: Botching the Die Hard reference in the most Charlie way possible
jokes.explanation: Charlie's incompetent attempt at action hero one-liner

jokes.text: This gun is rubber. Rubber gun! It's rubber? She's right. It's rubber. There's no police! There's no police down there, dude!
jokes.setup: Apparent victory over McPoyles
jokes.punchline: Discovering the entire hostage situation was fake
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection revealing the whole episode was an elaborate McPoyle prank

jokes.text: Whoo! Haha! Eat it, douche bags! Whoo! Whoo! McPoyles!
jokes.setup: McPoyles revealing their prank
jokes.punchline: Celebrating their elaborate revenge with childish taunts
jokes.explanation: Satisfying conclusion showing McPoyle dysfunction but also their competence at revenge
